AIDE4HF_schema_1_0.TEXT: y.o. female with past medical history of hypertension, dyslipidemia, hypothyroidism, heart failure-full details not available, was following up with a cardiologist here to establish care. Patient denies any chest pain chest pressure suggestive of angina. No prior history of CAD or arrhythmias. Patient has bilateral lower extremity edema but denies any dyspnea, orthopnea or PND. Blood pressures well controlled carvedilol and amlodipine and chlorthalidone/azilsartan combination pill. Patient has issues with balance issues and had few falls. Last creatinine was 1.1. Denies any other issues. The following portions of the chart were reviewed this encounter and updated as appropriate: Past Medical History Past Medical History: CNS: No focal neurological deficit Skin: Normal Cardiac Investigations EKG (PCP visit) Sinus bradycardia, heart rate 58 bpm, first-degree AV block, PVCs, left axis deviation, borderline poor R wave progression. Assessment/Plan 1. Heart failure Patient has history of heart failure-full details not available. Patient is here to establish care. Patient has bilateral lower extremity edema without any crackles or other heart failure symptoms. We have recommended her to use Lasix 40 mg p.o. daily for 3 to 5 days and as needed after that. Last creatinine was 1.1. Will obtain an echocardiogram to assess LVEF and valvular function. Further management depending upon echocardiogram results. 2. Hypertension Blood pressures well controlled on amlodipine 5 mg p.o. daily, carvedilol 12.5 mg p.o. twice daily and as losartan combination pill.

HFSymptoms.comment: Patient denies any chest pain chest pressure suggestive of angina.
Patient has bilateral lower extremity edema but denies any dyspnea, orthopnea or PND.

Vasodilator.comment: 2. Hypertension Blood pressures well controlled on amlodipine 5 mg p.o. daily, carvedilol 12.5 mg p.o. twice daily and as losartan combination pill.
